task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the specified criteria and handles the edge case of an empty input list correctly.
trace:
**
To solve this problem, I first ensure the list is sorted and contains only distinct values by converting it to a set and then sorting. I iterate through the list, identifying consecutive sequences of numbers. For each sequence, if the sequence has more than one number, I append the start and end of the range in 'start-end' format; otherwise, I append the single number. Finally, I join all parts with commas to form the result string. This approach ensures that the function handles the edge case of an empty list by returning an empty string and correctly formats ranges and individual numbers as specified.
